config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Mono-EN baseline: ID persona and US persona both speak English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Mono-EN baseline: ID persona and US persona both speak English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should automatically come before the rights of the individual, because once individual rights are treated as secondary, it becomes too easy for governments or majorities to justify abuse. In my view, a healthy society is built by protecting personal dignity, freedom, and due process first, then balancing those rights with the common good when there is a clear and limited reason. From my perspective, collective interests matter, but they should not be used as a blanket excuse to override basic rights.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The rights of the individual should come first, with society allowed to limit them only in narrow, clearly justified cases. From a United States perspective, history makes me wary of any broad claim that “society” should take priority, because that language has often been used to excuse discrimination, censorship, and abuses of power. At the same time, rights are not absolute in every situation: public safety, fraud prevention, and protecting others can justify limits, but those limits need strong legal checks and a high burden of proof. If we reverse the default and put society first, I think it becomes too easy to trample minority rights whenever the majority says it is for the common good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The rights of the individual should remain the default priority, because once “society” comes first, it becomes too easy to silence minorities and excuse abuse in the name of order. From my perspective in Indonesia, I have seen how appeals to harmony, public morality, or national interest can be used to pressure people into conformity, even when their basic dignity or due process is at stake. That does not mean society never matters, but collective interests should justify limits only in narrow, clear, and accountable cases such as public safety, not as a general principle. A strong society is built by protecting individuals well enough that the state and the majority cannot simply overpower them.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The rights of the individual should remain the default priority, because once “society” comes first, it becomes too easy to excuse censorship, discrimination, and abuse in the name of the common good. From a United States perspective, I am especially skeptical of vague appeals to social interest because our history shows how often majorities and governments have used them to suppress unpopular groups. Society does matter, but it should only override individual rights in narrow, clearly defined cases like preventing direct harm or protecting public safety, and even then with strong legal limits. If we put society ahead of the individual as a general rule, I think we invite exactly the kind of overreach that constitutional rights are supposed to prevent.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The rights of the individual should stay the default priority, because “society first” is too easily used to justify pressure, censorship, and abuse. From my perspective in Indonesia, I have seen how appeals to harmony, morality, or national interest can be used to make people stay silent even when their dignity or due process is being violated. Society absolutely matters, but it should only limit individual rights in narrow, clearly justified cases like preventing direct harm or maintaining public safety, with strict accountability. A healthy society is not one that can overpower individuals whenever it wants; it is one that protects people well enough that the majority cannot simply crush the minority.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The rights of the individual should come first, because in the United States I do not trust vague appeals to "society" to stay limited for long. History here shows that once the majority or the state gets to define the common good too broadly, it can easily become a cover for censorship, discrimination, and abuse against unpopular people. Society does matter, but it should only override individual rights in narrow cases where there is clear, direct harm and strong legal safeguards. If we make society the default priority, I think we weaken the very protections that keep minorities and dissenters safe.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
